name: Tier Builder
description: Subscription tier configuration interface with drag-reorder, benefit editor, pricing inputs, and live preview.

standard.description: Full tier editor with drag-reorder, benefits, and live preview

slots.tier-list: Existing tiers with drag handles for reordering
slots.tier-form: Create/edit tier form with all fields

compact.description: Simplified tier list view for mobile

visual_brief: Configuration interface for creating and managing subscription tiers. A tier list on the left shows existing tiers as draggable cards with name, price, and subscriber count. Selecting a tier opens an editor form on the right with fields: tier name input, price input (with currency selector), description textarea, and a benefits editor (sortable list of benefit items with add/remove). A live preview card shows how the tier will appear to subscribers. The compact preset combines the list and editor into a single-column flow. 'Add Tier' button at the bottom of the tier list.

responsive.mobile: Single-column layout — tier list at top, editor below when a tier is selected. Preview at the bottom. Drag-to-reorder uses long-press.
responsive.tablet: Two-column layout — tier list on left, editor on right. Preview below the editor.
responsive.desktop: Three-column layout — tier list, editor, and live preview all visible simultaneously.

layout_hints.builder_priority: The tier list establishes structure, the form enables editing, and the preview confirms the subscriber-facing result. Keep those roles visually distinct.
layout_hints.edit_focus: Selected tier context should remain obvious while editing benefits and pricing so users never lose track of which plan they are changing.
layout_hints.mobile_sequence: On narrow screens, preserve a stable top-to-bottom flow from list to editor to preview instead of forcing simultaneous multi-panel density.

motion.micro: Drag handles, selected tiers, and benefit rows may sharpen or highlight subtly during editing interactions.
motion.transitions: Tier reordering, selection changes, and preview updates should feel direct and workbench-like rather than playful.

keyboard: Tier selection, form fields, and any sortable benefits should remain operable by keyboard with clear focus and ordering cues.
announcements: Selected tier, pricing values, and benefit changes should be exposed semantically so plan editing remains understandable without drag interactions alone.

composition.TierForm: Form(d-surface) > [NameInput(d-control) + PriceInput(d-control, currency) + DescriptionTextarea(d-control) + BenefitsEditor > BenefitRow(d-data-row, sortable)[]]
